role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified the absence of linguistic cues (no signs) and stayed within domain.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Focused on terrain, vegetation, and climate.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Focused on plant types (bunchgrasses) and biome.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Focused on road markings and traffic rules.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized infrastructure and camera rig details.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Accurately described the arid high-plains terrain.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Correctly identified yellow center lines and right-hand traffic.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Correctly identified the camera rig shadow and fence styles.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents correctly identified the visual evidence (arid plains, yellow lines) but failed to distinguish between the US and Canada due to shared infrastructure and biome. The judge correctly resolved the tie using meta-evidence (camera rig) and regulatory nuance.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: The judge correctly identified a consensus on North America (Path A) and proceeded to country hypotheses. The reasoning was sound as all agents pointed to the same region.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool (US, Canada, Mexico) is plausible for the visual evidence. Mexico is a slight stretch given the specific road quality and vegetation, but the US/Canada distinction is the primary challenge here.
